summary.intent: Explore git-ai's capabilities, understand its data storage architecture, and evaluate whether it can serve as a data source for the team's existing metrics dashboard.
summary.outcome: git-ai was installed and tested end-to-end; a comprehensive reference doc was written to docs/git-ai-reference.md; the tool was determined to be local-first and unsuitable as a drop-in replacement for the current checkpoint system without extra per-developer setup.

repo: The metrics dashboard fetches checkpoint data from the `entire/checkpoints/v1` branch via GitHub API (github-checkpoints.ts + fetch-checkpoints.ts), not from local CLI.
git-ai stores attribution data as git notes under refs/notes/ai — one blob per commit, with path structure <first-2-chars-of-sha>/<remaining-sha>.
git-ai's local SQLite databases live at ~/.git-ai/internal/db (4 tables) and prompts.db is generated on demand in the repo root.
The note JSON has an empty `messages: []` field — full conversation transcripts are stored only in ~/.git-ai/internal/db and never travel with the git notes.

git-ai tracks: agent_percentage, agent_lines, human_added, files_touched, session_id, model, prompt_txt, checkpoint_id, commit_date. It does NOT track: tokens (input/output/cache), summaries, learnings, human_removed, bmad_commands.

code.finding: Fetches checkpoint metadata from entire/checkpoints/v1 branch via GitHub API — same pattern could be reused to fetch git-ai notes from refs/notes/ai.
CI/build script that populates the checkpoints cache; equivalent script would be needed for git-ai note aggregation.
A temporary test marker was added and then removed during the session to validate git-ai attribution tracking.
New file created: comprehensive reference covering all git-ai commands, SQLite schemas, git notes JSON structure, and gap analysis vs CheckpointMeta.

workflow: git notes are NOT pushed by default — developers must run `git push origin refs/notes/ai` explicitly, or configure `git config --global remote.origin.push '+refs/notes/ai:refs/notes/ai'`.
GitHub API traversal for git notes requires 3+N calls: ref → commit → recursive tree → N blob fetches. The recursive=1 tree fetch collapses note paths into one call.
git-ai's `--json` and `--verbose` flags are mutually exclusive — using both causes an error.
git-ai's `--since` flag on `--pattern` search is not yet implemented (warns but does not error).
prompt_storage config accepts `default`, `notes`, or `local` — all local; no remote DB connection string support.
After `git-ai login`, the CAS sync queue may auto-upload data to usegitai.com SaaS, which is the intended team-wide metrics path.

friction: `--json` and `--verbose` flags are mutually exclusive in git-ai but this is not documented upfront — discovered by error.
`--since` on `--pattern` search silently does nothing (warns 'not yet implemented').
GitHub web UI does not display git notes natively — requires 4-level API traversal to read note content.
git notes do not push automatically with `git push` — requires explicit configuration or manual push per developer.
The test commit (3e2941c) was not pushed to remote main before pushing notes, so the note references a commit GitHub cannot resolve.
Full prompt/message data stays on the developer's local machine and is never exported via git notes, making team-wide prompt analytics impossible without SaaS login.
open_items: Test whether `git-ai login` + CAS sync queue actually uploads data to usegitai.com and what the team dashboard shows.
Make a test commit while logged in to verify sync queue picks up new data after authentication.
Push main branch so commit 3e2941c is visible on GitHub alongside its notes.
Decide whether to build a `fetchGitAiNotes()` function (Option 2 from the session) to pull line-attribution data into the existing dashboard alongside checkpoint data.
Evaluate if git-ai's SaaS team dashboard covers enough metrics to reduce reliance on the custom checkpoint system, or if both systems need to coexist.
Set up a post-push git hook or CI step to auto-push refs/notes/ai if the team adopts git-ai for attribution tracking.
